lesson_plan.lesson_summary: This lesson functions as a source-faithful Module 1 close and bridge into Module 2. It recaps the core innovator-mindset pillars, reinforces diagnosis-first and root-cause thinking, reminds learners of the 5 Whys, Opportunity Radar, and psychological safety, and then pivots to the central bridge claim that ideas alone are insufficient without leadership identity, communication skill, and political savvy. It concludes with a production-scoped closing-video plan grounded only in the provided storyboard and narration, without inferring unavailable motion assets.

cd_directive.creative_rationale: Choose visual-led to honor the closing video format (talking head + text overlay) and the bridge-to-Identity moment. The on-camera presence carries emotional momentum while concise overlays (“Module 1 Complete…”) and a simple bridge graphic anchor the transition without dense copy. High creative weighting supports metaphor and forward energy; adaptive density keeps overlays minimal so attention stays on Dr. Naik’s encouragement. This segment isn’t teaching tools (e.g., 5 Whys) but recapping mindset and priming leadership identity, so visuals and tone should do the heavy lifting. Static or simple overlay graphics suffice—no reliance on complex motion assets.

lesson_plan.lesson_summary: A concise closing bridge for Module 1 that synthesizes completed mindset work, reframes the learner from system reactor to system redesigner, argues that ideas alone are insufficient without leadership capacity, and previews Module 2 as the move from Mindset to Identity. The plan uses two clusters for the recap and the idea-to-leader hinge, with singleton opening and closing units to preserve the source's closing-video simplicity.

output.additional_instructions: Experience profile: visual-led. Creative rationale: Choose visual-led to honor the closing video format (talking head + text overlay) and the bridge-to-Identity moment. The on-camera presence carries emotional momentum while concise overlays (“Module 1 Complete…”) and a simple bridge graphic anchor the transition without dense copy. High creative weighting supports metaphor and forward energy; adaptive density keeps overlays minimal so attention stays on Dr. Naik’s encouragement. This segment isn’t teaching tools (e.g., 5 Whys) but recapping mindset and priming leadership identity, so visuals and tone should do the heavy lifting. Static or simple overlay graphics suffice—no reliance on complex motion assets. Slide mode proportions: creative=0.6, literal-text=0.15, literal-visual=0.25.
